DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Matt White, Esq., of Matt White Law PC. Tel:415.226.4040 for plaintiff(via Zoom Conference)
Curtis Smolar, Esq., of GORDON REES Tel:415.964.9932 for defendant.

Hearing is held. Defendant counsel contests the tentative ruling overruling demurrer to Plaintiff's first amended complaint. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT NEIL EISENBERG'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Eisenberg Law Office, A Professional Corporation's demurrer to all three causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Carissa Copeland is overruled in its entirety. All four of the arguments of the Eisenberg Law Office (ELO) lack merit.

ELO's first argument asserting that the first cause of action for declaratory relief is time-barred lacks merit because the Doe Amendment naming ELO relates back to the filing of this case.

ELO's second argument that the first cause of action for declaratory relief fails to state a claim because it does not allege lack of adequate remedy or urgency lacks merit because no such allegations are required for a declaratory relief claim.

ELO's third argument that the second and third cause of action do not allege damages lack merit because both of those claims adequately allege damages.

ELO's fourth argument that the first amended complaint does not allege damages sufficient to support a superior court's civil unlimited jurisdiction lacks merit because that is not an argument cognizable on a demurrer and in all events the allegation that ELO asserts a lien of $80,000 is sufficient to support civil unlimited jurisdiction.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. MATTHEW WHITE, Esq. of Matt White Law PC (tel: 415-226-4020) for Plaintiff Carissa Copeland.
Atty. NEIL EISENBERG, Esq. of Eisenberg Law Office (tel: 415-956-3567) for Defendant Neil D. Eisenberg.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CARISSA COPELAND's Motion For Discovery Sanctions.

The defendant contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen, and sign the stipulation. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

The motion is granted. Neil D. Eisenberg to pay sanctions to plaintiff Copeland under C.C.P. section 2025.420(h) in the amount of $4,225.

Counsel Matthew White is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEIL EISENBERG'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

The lion's share of the money at issue in this case is an attorney fee "from or lien against" plaintiff's "recovery from" an underlying case involving a 2014 traffic accident. (FAC para. 26; Copeland v. Behre CGC-16-550659.) The underlying case is on appeal, so we do not yet know whether there will ultimately be a recovery. I therefore exercise my inherent power to stay this case pending resolution of the Copeland v. Behre appeal. (OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
